meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap in your SaaS knowledge with this high-level starter kit guide. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver professional SaaS apps faster.
content.answer_block: Stop reinventing the wheel. A robust SaaS starter kit abstracts away auth, billing, and database migrations, allowing you to focus on unique business logic. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative development, you can ship production-ready SaaS applications in days, not months, regardless of your background.
content.intro:
The market doesn't care about your technical debt; it cares about speed. You're a freelancer staring at a blank terminal, knowing that building a SaaS from scratch is a death trap of auth bugs and payment integration failures. The pressure to deliver is mounting, and the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier feels like a concrete wall between you and your next high-ticket contract.

But the wall is an illusion. The shift from 'coder' to 'SaaS Architect' happens the moment you stop writing boilerplate and start orchestrating systems. By deploying a battle-tested starter kit and automating your development flow with Claude Code, you bypass the grunt work that kills most projects. It’s time to move from manual labor to high-velocity shipping.

steps.title: Architecting the Base Structure
steps.description: Establish a scalable directory structure that separates concerns between core business logic and infrastructure overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular SaaS project structure using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include folder separation for 'services', 'hooks', and 'components'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure based on Antigravity architecture specs'

steps.title: Automating Authentication Flows
steps.description: Implement enterprise-grade auth without manual boilerplate by leveraging established middleware patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication middleware that handles session validation and redirects for protected routes using NextAuth and Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement middleware.ts for protected route auth with Supabase'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe Billing
steps.description: Abstract the complexity of webhooks and subscription tiers into a reusable service layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe service module that includes methods for subscription creation, customer lookup, and webhook verification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate Stripe webhook handler and subscription service logic'

steps.title: Database Schema Design
steps.description: Define a type-safe schema that allows for rapid iteration and data integrity across your SaaS features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a SaaS application including User, Subscription, and Project entities with proper relational mapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Sync schema.prisma with user and subscription models'

steps.title: Frontend UI Component Library
steps.description: Standardize your UI components so you can build new features by assembling pre-built blocks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of reusable Tailwind-based UI components including Buttons, Inputs, and Cards that follow a cohesive design system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate modular UI components using Radix UI and Tailwind'

pro_tips: Always build your database schema before your API routes to ensure type safety from the ground up.
Use mock data factories to test your billing webhooks locally before ever touching the Stripe dashboard.
Keep your Claude Code context windows clean by deleting unused log files and node_modules from the prompt scope.
Standardize your error handling across the entire app into a single global middleware to save hours of debugging.
Treat your starter kit as a product; iterate on it every time you finish a client project to make the next one 20% faster.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is functional, deploy it to Vercel and create a private repository template so you can clone it for every future client project.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to scale your freelance business and stop wasting time on repetitive engineering tasks. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
